page: Amazon Web Services c8in.32xlarge is a compute-optimized and network-optimized eighth-generation instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. The server features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 dedicated physical cores with hyperthreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. Operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, the CPU configuration is paired with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

The network architecture of this instance is highly optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This high-throughput network interface is managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring low-latency virtualization overhead and dedicated resource allocation. The high network bandwidth makes it suitable for workloads requiring intensive node-to-node communication or high-speed access to Amazon Elastic Block Store volumes.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8in.32xlarge is positioned as a high-density compute resource. The lack of local NVMe storage and GPU accelerators reduces hardware overhead for workloads that rely purely on CPU processing and network-attached storage. This makes the instance a viable option for distributed compute clusters, network appliances, high-performance computing nodes, and large-scale batch processing where high network throughput and raw CPU capacity are the primary requirements.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.32xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized eighth-generation instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance includes no local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for high-throughput network tasks, distributed computing, and CPU-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, running on the Intel x86_64 architecture.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.32xlarge is an Intel-powered, compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, compute-optimized virtual server with dedicated Intel processors and high-throughput network performance for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical Intel cores running at 3.9 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and clustering
Powered by the low-overhead AWS Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Designed for high-performance computing, batch processing, and network appliances
